top_emotional_moments.text: Thank you, Vic. It's good to be here.
Well, it's great having you. Thank you for your time.
Kirk, please give us a brief bio on yourself.

And you mentioned your occupation. For the people who are complaining about that Best Of episode last Friday night, I had an interview scheduled with Kirk that I was going to air last Friday, but unfortunately had a mishap at work. What happened to you, Kirk?
Well, unfortunately, I was having to take a part out of a large industrial grinder.
And instead of pushing the wrench to loosen the bolt, I had to pull against it.
I had a lot of pressure on it.
Well, I'm still sore, but it's getting better.

segments.text: Thank you.
Thank you.
Tonight's guest is Kirk Reid.
Kirk, welcome to the show.
Thank you, Vic. It's good to be here.
Well, it's great having you. Thank you for your time.
Kirk, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
Well, 64-year-old welding engineer was exposed to cryptids at 18 years old with a sighting of a Bigfoot
and been researching into the cryptids ever since and have had many encounters.
The deeper I dig into them, the more different types of cryptids it seems that there are.
But a lot of my encounters have been with dog men.
It's funny how that works. It's a lot like an onion.
Once you start peeling back layers, you know that is.

And you mentioned your occupation. For the people who are complaining about that Best Of episode last Friday night, I had an interview scheduled with Kirk that I was going to air last Friday, but unfortunately had a mishap at work. What happened to you, Kirk?
Well, unfortunately, I was having to take a part out of a large industrial grinder.
I had an inch and 15-16ths wrench, which is about a little over two foot long.
And instead of pushing the wrench to loosen the bolt, I had to pull against it.
I had a lot of pressure on it.
The wrench slipped off, came back, hit me in the jaw, knocked two of my teeth out.
I actually swallowed one of them and fractured my jaw.
Oh, no.
How's your recovery?
Well, I'm still sore, but it's getting better.
Well, thank goodness for that.
Holy cow.
All things considered, how dangerous do you think dogmen are, Kirk?
I think they get, to some extent, a bad rap.
But let's face it, anything that has teeth and has claws can be a killer.
Would I want to go up against one?
No, not really.
I've had one within two and a half foot of me, maybe three foot at the most.
It growled at me, but then it turned around and ran away.
So it could have easily killed me right then, had it wanted to.
I'm not saying that these things are something you set around a campfire saying kumbaya with.
It's good to have a healthy respect for them.
but I don't think that they are as dangerous as people make them out to be.
They are not the werewolf, for the most part, that people associate with them.
I couldn't agree more with everything you said.
For some reason, people listen to the show and they get this impression that

I think you can sing Kumbaya with them and hang out with them and everything will be fine I never going to tell an eyewitness that dogmen are safe to be around I just bring light to the fact that when this eyewitness had an encounter where a dogman
was one step away from them, allowed that person to leave without a scratch, this eyewitness
over here had something very similar happen where the dogman had them dead to rights,
terrified them but never harmed them.
I'll tell those eyewitnesses, yes, that dogman, he had your dead rights, but he proved with his own actions that he didn't want to hurt you.
That's not to say that all dogmen are that way.
That is exactly right.
I've got no doubts there are dogmen out there that are just dangerous as all get out to be around.
I'm never going to say that's not the case, but unfortunately, there are quite a few people who listen to the show that have got that twisted for some reason.
So, just wanted to put that out there.
You put a lot of research into the Corey Godsey murder, Kirk.
For the people who don't know about that case, please talk us through that and any opinions you might have on what happened.
Well, Corey Godsey was a 13 and a half year old boy down in Kentucky, just south of Hazard, Kentucky, slightly.
and he went into his bedroom one day to do his homework when the mother called him for dinner.
Lo and behold, he wasn't there.
911 was called.
911 dispatched cars to look for him,
which was a very odd thing that they would dispatch cars so easily, so quickly.
But the law enforcement, the first responders, all searched for him, could not find him.
The next day, they actually researched on the same area that they did,
And they found him about a quarter mile behind his house, up the hill, dead, partially consumed.
His stomach and his intestinal area was where the attack, shall we say, was concentrated.
For lack of a better term, they defined it as canine injuries.
The sheriff, I'm sorry, the police chief of the town actually said that it was a pack of wild dogs.
they caught five quote wild dogs and had them put down right away without testing them
one dog was a french bulldog which was 13 pounds there were two corgis which were each about 21 to
22 pounds there was a standard poodle that weighed almost 40 pounds and then there was a
Labrador Retriever, which was a 60-pound animal. All of them are a good-natured breed,
and all of them appeared to be good-natured dogs when they captured them, but yet those are the
animals that were blamed on a supposedly drug quarry up the side of this hill a quarter mile,
and I was there. I walked up the hill. Don't get me wrong, it's not an easy walk,
but it would be an impossibility for all five of those dogs combined to drag Corey up that hill.
They just would not have the strength required.
And on top of that, there were no drag marks at all.
It was as if something carried him up there.
And as I say, he was not found the day that he disappeared.
He was not found until the next day in the area that they had already searched.
so where he was between the initial search and the second search when they located the body is
totally beyond me outside of his bedroom window and the bedroom window was open there were large
canine prints seen when i say large they were approximately six inches in diameter which puts it out of the realm of being any domesticated dog puts it out of the realm of being any normal wolf
So with that being said, there's pretty much only one thing that leaves a canine print that size,
and that would be a dog man.
Can you imagine what that poor kid went through? Wow.

I can't even imagine it. The fear in him had to be just astronomical. And then he was consumed alive. They determined he was still alive during the attack on his midsection and his intestines being eaten.
Horrible doesn't do it justice. Poor kid.
I remember when that happened and all the buzz about that when it happened, but it didn't take very long before the buzz died off.
I wonder if it was intentionally hushed or it was natural.
Nope. I was actually, when I was down there poking into things, digging around, I was actually unofficially escorted out of the city by a police officer.
I was told there was nothing there to see, nothing there to find, that they had taken care of everything, and that they don't take kindly to people noticing around.
But I can tell you this, to this day, they are still picking up any dog that they deem as a stray and destroying immediately.

And the reason I actually started looking into the Godsey case, I was somewhat aware of it, but a lady called me from the dog pound, and she wanted me to look into this and see if I could potentially prove that it was not stray dogs,
because they were killing all these dogs unjustly.
And when I found out about all the dogs that had been killed,
even after those five dogs,
that's when I decided that there had to be a lot more to it.
And so I took a trip down there and spent four and a half days.
You're not easy to deter.
That's for sure.
That's admirable.
You started researching cryptids in 1977 after a Bigfoot sighting you had.
Recently we recorded an episode of My Bigfoot Sighting where you talked about that sighting
as well as other Bigfoot sightings you've had.
On that show, Kirk talks about that first sighting as well as an encounter you had with
a juvenile that grabbed him by his ankle and gave him injuries that he still suffers from
present day.
That episode of My Bigfoot Sighting is going to drop tonight at the same time this episode
of Dogman Encounters did.
If you'd like to check out that episode, please go to the description for tonight's show and you'll find a link to it.
You know of two boys who killed a dogman in 2012, Kirk.
What did they tell you about how their experience played out?
Well, they were actually out deer hunting before deer season.
They were going to what we call jack-a-deer, which means poach-a-deer,
simply because they were both from poor families.
They were out on ATVs.
One was armed with a 1100 shotgun, which is a semi-automatic.
The other was armed with a 870 shotgun, which is a pump.
They went at night, about 1.30 in the morning, to a field where they knew deer were known to graze.
And they parked their ATVs.
they got off them they started to walk into the field because they saw eyes they were going to
sneak up on these deer and see if they couldn't get a couple as they started to sneak up on the deer
the one decided he would use the spotlight on them and a lot of times if you hit a deer with
the spotlight, it'll just freeze and stand there for a minute.
You have a very easy time getting a shot off.
That's why spotlighting deer is illegal.
But when he shined the spotlight on it, lo and behold, it wasn't a deer.
It was about an eight and a half to nine foot tall dog man.
And it started to charge at him.
When it got within about 80 yards of them they both started firing at it
They expended a total of 13 rounds of deer slugs.
They hit it eight times before it finally was down,
and it literally fell.
The last two shots they put in it,
it was less than 15 foot away from them.
Now, they went back to town.
They got a buddy of theirs after some coaxing and convincing
that they actually had just killed a monster
and if he would take his four-wheel drive truck with the wench out there and retrieve the body,
they could all be millionaires.
When they got out there to retrieve the body,
five black SUVs followed them in and they were from the government.
They told them, took each one into a SUV separately,
read them the riot act told them they were going to put them in jail they were going to put their
families in jail they were going to take their hunting rights forever they were going to just
everything you can imagine to terrorize a 17 year old kid makes you wonder what was more traumatic
for them having that eight to nine foot tall dogman charging them or what followed after with
suits well quite frankly when i talked to the one the other one ended up od'ing but every time i
talked to the one he brings up the black suvs more than he does the dog man and as he says it's not
so much that it was traumatizing, that it absolutely made him so mad he could spit nails,
that the government knows about these, the government was able to respond,
and the government will not tell people about them.
And as he always brings up the question,
We told nobody. How did they know?
And it's a valid question.
That is a valid question.
A lot of people propose the idea that maybe these guys have chips in them,
and when they're killed, maybe somehow a signal is sent out.
It does make you wonder.
And I've thought the same thing at times.
I mean, I'm not sure.
but I can tell you also that dogmen have been around for thousands of years
so they're not anything new
so I would have to assume or guess shall we say
that the ones that have chips in them
of course are ones that our government has altered
or possibly even raised
and then there's always the dogman that you find out in the wilderness, shall we call it,
that is just a normal dogman.
Now, which one of the two is the most dangerous?
I don't know.
You raise some really good points.
It cracks me up when people pose the idea that dogmen are just something that's been created in a test tube
and that explains the whole phenomenon.
But like I said, they've been around so much longer than we've had the technology to come close to genetically splice DNA and do what would be required to actually produce a dogman in a laboratory.
So, yeah, I think there are two different directions we're talking about here.
You've got the dogmen that have been around the whole time.
Then you probably do have some that are straight out of a test tube.
Yep.
and that's truthfully exactly what I believe.
The very sad fact is when you do see one, you don't know which it is.
Yeah, that's a good point.
In 1982, you heard about the werewolf of London, Ohio, Kirk.
How did you hear about it and what can you tell us about it?
Okay, the werewolf of London, Ohio was said to be a werewolf that was stalking the local area.
London, Ohio is kind of southwestern Ohio, somewhere down around Dayton.
Anyways, I...
I'd heard about it because the, quote, werewolf, who they said was the werewolf, actually died.
And then the werewolf disappeared after this man died.
Well, I was, at that point in time, still trying to wrap my head around the Bigfoot thing.
and I actually had read an article, just a small article in, I want to say,
the Cleveland Press or Cleveland Plain Dealer, one of those two papers,
and I figured I'm going to go down there and talk to the people,
see if what I saw matches what they saw.
I don't think this was a werewolf I saw, but maybe it is.
So that was in the late fall that I encountered that newspaper article.
Then in the spring of 83 is when I went down there and I talked to people.
Some people did not want to talk.
Some people said that they didn't believe it.
I did get to talk to half a dozen people that actually had seen it,
and I had talked to the owner of the gas station that the guy worked at that they blamed for being the werewolf.
all the owner of the gas station could say was well the man showed up one day wanting a job
i had an opening so i hired him that he really didn't like to be around any people
and that we do enough business with tires tire repairs and such that i was able to keep him back
in a mechanic shop just fixing tires and such.
I didn't have to pay him a lot.
Don't know where he lived.
He would walk to work.
He'd be here when we got here, and he would walk home.
We offered him rides.
He would never accept them.
And everybody that I talked to that actually saw the werewolf in the werewolf form,
there was no doubt it was a dog man.
was not what I had seen that, like I said, I ended up finding out was Sasquatch, Bigfoot, whatever you want to call it.
So that was the start of the second cryptid now I had to research.
And, you know, it's hard enough researching one, trying to concentrate on it.
Now you've got your interest peaked down a second one, and you can't let go of the first one.
So now you spend twice as much time doing it.
Yeah, it's, shall we say, addictive maybe.
You want that knowledge.
But the house that George the werewolf lived in
And I'm going to call him George because I really don't remember his name
But the house that he lived in had one room in it
That was actually lined with 5-8 thick steel plate the entire room
Now, the police, when they went to do a, shall we call it, well-being check on this man
because he had not shown up for work for a few days, and they had an idea where he lived.
When they found the house and went in, and they found him in there, they also found the room.
There were scratches, deep scratches in that solid steel plate.
that could not, quote, be made by any animal.
So it seems like somebody was actually helping this man
and was trying to control him when he would change into the werewolf.
They seemed to have been locking him in this room,
and the scratches were made by him trying to get out of the room.
but nobody ever knew who the helper was although I did get a very good description of a red lady
Never could find her for sure, but...
And of course, when he died, she disappeared.
Thank goodness for that room, huh?
It does make you wonder if there were ever any nights when maybe he was out when he transformed.
Hope that's not the case, but you never know.
Well, there's, without a doubt, there's nights that he was out.
He was actually tracked back to this house by a neighbor that lived about a mile and a half away
that he would, as a werewolf, be over basically terrorizing him.
and they tracked his tracks right back to this house,
and that actually was how the police knew where this house was to go do the well-being check.
That's right.
Yeah, the couple's names were Keith and Diane, if I remember correctly.
Yeah, I believe you do.
Can you imagine how bad it would be to have a werewolf lurking around your property like that?
dogmen are bad enough, but
to actually have a bonafide werewolf,
that's not good.
Yeah, exactly.
If you've had a dogman encounter and would like
to speak with me about it, whether in private
or on the show, please go to
dogmanencounters.com and
submit a report. If you've had
a Bigfoot sighting and would like to be a guest
on one of my two Bigfoot shows,
please go to mybigfootsighting.com
and let me know.
Alright, Kurt, please tell us about
Watch your own encounters now.
Give us every last detail that comes to mind.
Okay, my very first dogman encounter.
I had got a new 2008 Jeep Liberty.
It was exactly perfect what I wanted.
I took it over right after picking it up and went over to my buddy's house to show him.
As I'm over there, he has a very long driveway.
It's about 100 yards long.
It is only one lane wide.
There is no place to turn around.
So if you pull in the driveway, you have to back back out.
As you're backing out, there are woods to the left of you.
and a big open field to the right of you.
So after I got done showing off my new ride,
I was backing out.
And in the trees, I kind of catch a glimpse of something moving.
Didn't think anything of it.
Thought it was a deer.
We have plenty of deer here in Pennsylvania.
So I just, I continued backing out.
As I get to the end of the driveway, I cut my wheels to go out onto the road, stop, start to throw the car, and drive.
and lightning quick this thing comes and literally jumped across the hood of that jeep
landed right beside the driver's door of it i'm sitting there with the window down
its face is looking in at me about two and a half maybe three foot away it kind of growls and shows
its teeth and i'm just absolutely frozen i'm not knowing what to do you know the smart thing to do
would have been throw it and drive and stomp on the throttle but at that point in time you know
i'm not even knowing what to do uh like i say it shows its teeth kind of growls turns
takes two steps. The first step was across the road onto the embankment. The second step was
up the embankment to the top of it, and then it just jumped, and it was back into the woods.
And there I am, still just sitting there trying to figure out what just happened.
you know how do you process something like that
so that was my absolute first face to face with a dog man So now after the Sasquatch after finding out about the werewolf of London Ohio
now after seeing a dog man for myself in person so close I could have reached out and touched him,
I could feel his breath against me.
I will never forget the smell of his breath, but that's regardless.
So now that just gives me more.
I've got to search more.
I've got to look and start really going out, doing research.
Over the years, I've had several different partners that were research partners with me.
the second encounter happened down in Braxton County, West Virginia.
As me and the guy that was with me, we were walking into this one holler,
and literally we heard a growl.
And when I say a growl, it sounded exactly like a dog, only like through a megaphone, amplified a hundred times.
I mean, the lung power on it had to be astronomical.
that one we were we were in a way fortunate because we didn't have an encounter as per se
other than the growl and that was a very good thing because as nasty as the growl sounded i
probably would not have wanted to run into that one
And moving forward, I was at a location about 30 miles from my house in slightly northern Pennsylvania.
Was out there scouting for deer hunting areas.
Had permission from the property owner.
I was walking down this one trail
up in front of me about 75 foot
there lays a gray wolf, I'm thinking.
And I mean, very large gray wolf,
which shouldn't be here in Pennsylvania.
so
of course I froze in my tracks
and I was unarmed
had a knife on me
that was about it because it wasn't
hunting season yet or anything
and I was not
expecting anything to be out there
so
I figure
okay I'm just
going to back away slowly
you know
I don't want to do the flight thing and then become prey.
So I start to back away.
And this thing gets up on all fours like a normal wolf.
Like it's going to, you know, I'm thinking maybe it's going to come at me or maybe I'll be lucky and it's going to head the other way.
Well, then from four legs, it stands up to two.
Turns and thank God it walked away from me instead of at me.
and there again, as soon as it started walking away, I started running.
You know, there is no valor when there's no reason to try to challenge one.
If you can get away from it, you're best off.
Now, probably one of the scariest ones, and not just for me, but for the dog man also, just happened a couple years ago, maybe four or five years ago.
me and a very good friend, Hank, he was a researcher with me, actually helped
found Northeast Cryptid Investigations. We were down at one of our standard
investigating areas It was a cool rainy day Not much was going on so we had brought our four with us
and we decided we'd go out for a little ride. Well Hank does like to drink beer and
And that's not a good thing, usually, riding a four-wheeler and drinking beer.
But he was.
Well, when we get back to camp, it's approximately 9.30, 10 o'clock, maybe a little later.
The fires went out.
so we had brought two five-gallon cans of gasoline for the four wheelers and a five-gallon can of
diesel fuel to use to start the fires with well as i say hank had had a few too many beers i guess
And instead of grabbing the can of diesel fuel, he grabbed a gas can and poured about three gallons of gasoline on what was going to become the fire and then lit it.
Well, it blew up.
We had flames shooting 30, 35 foot in the air.
There was a very large clump of weeds just off to the, what would have been, would have been my left, his right.
But there was the large clump of weeds.
when this fire blew up, a dogman jumped up on two legs that was hiding behind this clump of weeds
and took off running, snapping four-inch diameter branches off of the trees
as it ran up the hillside through the woods.
And we really did not want to follow it because it was dark, didn't follow it as per se.
We went up, looked a little bit, says, OK, it's gone.
We didn't get any sleep that night to speak of, even though we took turns, you know, each one staying awake to be on guard.
and let the other one sleep still.
When it was your turn to sleep, you didn't sleep.
In the morning, when it was daylight,
then we actually went and saw what kind of damage it was able to do.
And the one that sticks out in my mind the most is the fact
There was a black walnut tree that had a four-inch diameter branch at about six and a half foot off the ground,
and this thing snapped that off like it was a twig when it ran through.
The amount of power it would have taken to snap a four-inch branch off a black walnut,
But it's unfathomable that a human could do it.
And like I said, we both saw clear as day that this was no human.
This was a dog man.
It was probably seven and a half to eight foot tall.

I have had maybe four or five others, but they've just been, shall we say, minor sightings where I'll be out in the woods and you can hear something and you just catch a glimpse of it, that it's trailing you.
but you really don't have an encounter.
The other real good encounter was I had a guest researcher out of Ohio down a couple years ago
who thought she wanted to see a dog man, thought she was ready to see a dog man.
She was on the back of my four-wheeler.
we were going to get cigarettes because her and her fiance hadn't brought any
cigarettes even though they smoke.
So there was a very old barn right beside the trail we were going down.
The door was not what you would call open, not really open.
off the door was kind of hanging it was one of those sliding doors and it was kind of hanging
one hinge was on one hinge was off to where the door would only maybe open about seven or eight
foot instead of the probably 15 foot that it did and because of the type of trail it was
And where we were, we were only going maybe 15 mile an hour.
I am the type that I'm always looking around.
I keep my head on a swivel, shall we say, as I am out and about, because you never know what you're going to see.
And this guest researcher, she was doing the same as we come up to this barn.
And lo and behold, this barn's only 15 foot from the edge of the trail.
And there is a big black dog man ready to pounce on us because we were on the four-wheeler.
I hit the gas as hard as it would go.
We got out of there.
That was fortunate.
Like I say, it was ready to pounce.
And I was not ready to be dinner.
But I believe that might have been what it had in mind.
And there again, it may have been a bluff charge, but it did come out after us.
Like I say, I hit the throttle on that four-wheeler just as hard as I could.
And we actually got up to about 62 mile an hour getting away from it.
and until we were above about 40 mile an hour, it was still catching us.
If that isn't a brown shorts moment, I don't know what is.
Yeah, exactly.
Well, for the guest researcher, it was definitely a wet shorts moment.
Oh, no, I believe it, and rightfully so.
Yeah, I mean, that particular one,
And I can't say it wasn't a bluff charge, but, you know, we were already by it.
It would have had no reason to do a bluff charge to us.
That's why I don't believe it had any good intentions.
In my opinion, if it really wanted you, it could have very easily allowed you to go past it, and it could have come up behind you.
Or you never would have had a chance to make a run for it in the first place.
But that's just my opinion.
Right.
and that's the possibility too we'll never know that one way or another for sure that's a good
point when that dog being luke duke did across the hood of your liberty did it cause any damage
no it cleared the hood completely i mean literally do you remember that old show the six million
dollar man. Oh yeah, I used to watch it all the time. Okay, well, the way it jumped was almost
the way the six million dollar man jumped. I mean, it just massive leg strength,
and it cleared the top of the hood of the Jeep by probably a good two and a half foot easily.
and surprisingly the way it landed it was not awkward it did not stumble when it landed Its feet were perfectly just I guess you say a perfect landing
How tall would you say that one was?
That was a good seven and a half foot.
You know, at times looking back, I question, was it really that big?
And then at times I stop and think, well, when it turned and bounded away, you know, it stood up and literally the Jeep is pretty tall.
And basically from the armpits up was above the roof of the Jeep.
So it probably was about seven, seven and a half feet tall then.
Oh, yeah.
What more can you tell us about its appearance?
Its appearance, it was a very dark brown, not solid colored, shall we say.
It had some splotchiness to it where it almost looked like the fur was thinner in some areas than others.
Almost as if you could see skin through the fur.
Like maybe it had manes or something, or like it had been fighting with something and lost some fur.
Arms were very long.
It had not really hands, but hands kind of like a raccoon's.
It's hair.
It stunk, let me put it to you like that.
Its hair in some spots were matted, in some spots were not matted.
Like it really could have used a good trip to a dog grill murder, shall we say.
It had a pretty big upper body.
Its waist was narrow.
for the size of its shoulders its legs were bent what we call a backwards bend like a dog's leg is
you know it just it it it was puzzling oh i'm sure it was what did you notice about its teeth though
there were lots of them they seemed to be very large and they were very pointy not like a dog's
teeth it okay a dog has basically the two canines top and bottom well this had four upper canines
and to me that was
shall we say just that much more scary
because that takes it kind of out of being
well let's just say you don't know what it is
but on the other hand
looking back at it and thinking about other things

his teeth very much did mimic what a coyote teeth are like Only a whole lot larger Yeah I sure that a sight you never going to forget Do you think all dogmen are flesh and blood creatures or do you think some might be supernatural beings
I think all dogmen are flesh and blood creatures.
now with that being said
I'm not going to say that
a dog man and a werewolf are the same thing
because I don't think they are
and an actual werewolf
I believe is a shapeshifter
the Native Americans believe that
and because of the fact I am part Native American
And, you know, I have to go along with their beliefs for the simple fact that they've dealt with these things for thousands of years.
Native Americans know so much more about dogmen, Sasquatch, you name it than we're ever going to know.
So they've forgotten more than we're ever going to know about them.
Oh, exactly right.
Do you think dogmen are responsible for most of the missing person cases, Kirk?
I wouldn't say most of them.
I would say Dogman account for a portion of them.
I believe Sasquatch accounts for a portion of them.
And there's other things out there that we've seen that I don't even know what they are.
And I would have to assume they account for a portion.
And there again on some of the missing persons cases,
I believe that they may be criminal activity and that the police just haven't found the bodies.
You know, I don't think you can lump anyone into any one category.
It's a combination of all.
